summary: PID sheet URL fix fully verified. All 51 tests pass: old broken fragments (info-andcontent) and old version numbers (v1782931775/777/780) are gone from all 6 pre-rendered files; new URLs (v1784556231/219/207 + info-and-content path) are exact and in-order in root index.html and present in all CR1/CR2/CR3 pre-rendered files; all three Cloudinary PDFs return HTTP 200 with application/pdf content-type; preview URL serves the corrected hrefs on /cr1, /cr2, /cr3; regressions (JSON-LD offers, inches-first dimensions, rotomold FAQ answer, dual-voltage 120V/240V, _redirects=20 rules, _headers=15 blocks, sitemap=6 urls) all clean.
critical_code_review_comments: No issues found. URL swap is clean, all pre-rendered outputs are in sync with source, and the Cloudinary assets are live and PDF-typed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_pid_urls.py covers three product-page PID download hrefs. If versioned Cloudinary URLs are rotated again, only update the EXPECTED_URLS dict at the top of the file. Live Cloudinary checks add ~2s and preview route checks hit REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — if the preview URL changes, override REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env or edit the constant.
rca of the issue: Original bug had two overlapping errors in the pid-download-btn href: (1) stale Cloudinary version numbers (v1782931775/777/780) causing 404s after asset re-upload, and (2) a path-segment typo 'websites-product-info-andcontent' missing the hyphen between 'and' and 'content'. Main agent replaced all three <a class="pid-download-btn"> hrefs in /app/frontend/index.html (lines 2013, 2256, 2469) with the new v1784556231/219/207 URLs and correct 'info-and-content' segment, then re-ran node build-static.js to regenerate cr1/cr2/cr3 pre-rendered outputs. Verified live: all three PDFs now 200 OK from Cloudinary and served correctly on the preview URL.
